tools.name: forge_ls
tools.description: List roots, folder contents, or a file's sections. No argument: list roots. Folder path (ending with /): list subfolders and files. File path: list top-level sections.

path.description: Optional. Omit to list roots. Folder path (ending with /) to list its contents. File path to list its sections.

tools.name: forge_mkdir
tools.description: Create a folder. Error if it already exists.

tools.name: forge_rmdir
tools.description: Delete a folder. Error if not empty.

tools.name: forge_move
tools.description: Move a file or folder to a new path. Error if destination already exists. Moving across roots is not supported.

target.description: Destination path. Must be within the same root.

tools.name: forge_rename
tools.description: Rename a file or folder in place.

path.description: Path of the file or folder to rename.

name.description: New name (not a full path — name only).

tools.name: forge_read
tools.description: Read a file's content as structured JSON. Strips boilerplate. Large rarely-needed sections (changelog, history) return headers only by default — use the query parameter to load their content. Returns a format field identifying the file's format; native files return format: "<extension>".

path.description: File path. Standard filesystem path or forge:// URI.

query.description: Optional. Dot-notation path to a specific section. Examples: 'changelog', 'changelog.20260112', 'why'. If omitted, returns full content with lazy sections as headers only.

tools.name: forge_write
tools.description: Write changes to an existing file. Send only what changed — the rest is untouched. Use _action to insert, delete, or reorder sections and records.

path.description: File path. The file must already exist — use forge_create first.

payload.description: Partial JSON matching the structure returned by forge_read. Only included sections are modified. Use _action for structural operations: insert, delete, reorder.

tools.name: forge_create
tools.description: Create a new file with the specified format. Writes the metadata block and an empty skeleton. To know available formats, read forge://registry.

path.description: File path. Error if the file already exists.

format.description: Format name from the registry. Use the native format name (extension) for unstructured files. Consult forge_read('forge://registry') first if the format is not already known from context.

payload.description: Optional. Initial content to populate the skeleton. Must contain only implicit update actions.

tools.name: forge_delete
tools.description: Delete a file. Irreversible. To delete folders, use forge_rmdir.

path.description: File path. Error if the file does not exist.
